name: Menu and Menubar
description: A menu offers a list of choices (actions or functions), while a menubar is a visually persistent, typically horizontal menu providing consistent access to commands.

keyboard_interactions.action: Opens submenu and focuses first item; or activates item and closes menu
(Optional) Toggles menuitemcheckbox; checks menuitemradio; opens submenu; or activates item
In menubar: opens submenu. In menu: moves focus to next item
In menu: moves focus to previous item. In menubar (optional): opens submenu, focuses last item
In menubar: moves to next item. In submenu: opens child submenu or closes current and moves to next menubar item
In menubar: moves to previous item. In submenu: closes submenu, returns to parent
Closes the menu and returns focus to invoking element
(Optional) Moves focus to next item beginning with that character

required_attributes.description: false when hidden; true when visible

notes: Menus are for application-style actions, not site navigation — use <nav> for navigation
Focus management uses roving tabindex or aria-activedescendant
Only the first menubar item should have tabindex='0'; all others should be -1
